# scm_forwarding_profile_list (Data Source)

Retrieves a listing of config items.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# This data source will call the "ListForwardingProfiles" API endpoint
# and return all forwarding profiles in the "Mobile Users" folder.

# 1. Use a single data block to fetch ALL forwarding profiles in the "Mobile Users" folder.
data "scm_forwarding_profile_list" "all_mobile_users" {
  folder = "Mobile Users"
}

# 2. Use a 'for' expression to transform the list into a map.
# This creates a map where the key is the forwarding profile id and the value is the profile object.
output "forwarding_profile_data_source_results_from_list" {
  description = "A map of all forwarding profile objects in the Mobile Users folder, keyed by id."
  value       = { for fp in data.scm_forwarding_profile_list.all_mobile_users.data : fp.id => fp }
}

data "scm_forwarding_profile_list" "paginated_profiles_example" {
  folder = "Mobile Users"
  limit  = 5
  offset = 0
}

output "paginated_forwarding_profiles" {
  description = "A list of 5 forwarding profile names, skipping none - 0 offset"
  value       = { for fp in data.scm_forwarding_profile_list.paginated_profiles_example.data : fp.id => fp }
}

output "pagination_forwarding_profiles_details" {
  description = "Details about the pagination."
  value = {
    total_objects_in_folder = data.scm_forwarding_profile_list.paginated_profiles_example.total
    limit_used              = data.scm_forwarding_profile_list.paginated_profiles_example.limit
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `device` (String) The device of the item.
- `folder` (String) The folder of the item. Default: Shared.
- `limit` (Number) The max number of items to return. Default: 200.
- `name` (String) The name of the item.
- `offset` (Number) The offset of the first item to return.
- `snippet` (String) The snippet of the item.

### Read-Only

- `data` (Attributes List) The data. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data))
- `tfid` (String) The Terraform ID.
- `total` (Number) The total number of items.

<a id="nestedatt--data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data`

Required:

- `id` (String) The UUID of the forwarding profile

Optional:

- `folder` (String) The folder in which the resource is defined
- `name` (String) forwarding profile name as an alphanumeric string [ 0-9a-zA-Z._ -]

Read-Only:

- `definition_method` (String) Enable forwarding rule for forwarding profile
- `description` (String) Forwarding profile description
- `tfid` (String) The Terraform ID.
- `type` (Attributes) Forwarding profile type configuration (PAC file, GlobalProtect proxy, or ZTNA agent) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type))

<a id="nestedatt--data--type"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type`

Read-Only:

- `global_protect_proxy` (Attributes) Global Protect proxy-based forwarding configuration

> ℹ️ **Note:** You must specify exactly one of `global_protect_proxy`, `pac_file`, and `ztna_agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy))
- `pac_file` (Attributes) PAC file based forwarding configuration

> ℹ️ **Note:** You must specify exactly one of `global_protect_proxy`, `pac_file`, and `ztna_agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--pac_file))
- `ztna_agent` (Attributes) ZTNA agent-based forwarding configuration

> ℹ️ **Note:** You must specify exactly one of `global_protect_proxy`, `pac_file`, and `ztna_agent`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent))

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.global_protect_proxy`

Read-Only:

- `block_rule` (Attributes) Basic block rule configuration for PAC file and GlobalProtect proxy profiles (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule))
- `forwarding_rules` (Attributes List) List of GlobalProtect proxy-based forwarding rules (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--forwarding_rules))
- `pac_upload` (Boolean) User uploaded PAC file for Global Protect proxy-based forwarding configuration

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.global_protect_proxy.block_rule`

Read-Only:

- `allow_tcp` (Attributes) TCP traffic allowlist configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule--allow_tcp))
- `allow_udp` (Attributes) UDP traffic allowlist configuration with location and destination support (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule--allow_udp))
- `enable` (Boolean) Enable block rule

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule--allow_tcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.global_protect_proxy.block_rule.allow_tcp`

Read-Only:

- `enable_locations` (Boolean) Enable locations for allow-tcp
- `locations` (List of String) List of user locations allowed for TCP traffic


<a id="nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--block_rule--allow_udp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.global_protect_proxy.block_rule.allow_udp`

Read-Only:

- `destinations` (String) Destination addresses or networks allowed for UDP traffic
- `enable_destinations` (Boolean) Enable destinations for allow-udp
- `enable_locations` (Boolean) Enable locations for allow-udp
- `locations` (List of String) List of user locations allowed for UDP traffic



<a id="nestedatt--data--type--global_protect_proxy--forwarding_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.global_protect_proxy.forwarding_rules`

Read-Only:

- `connectivity` (String) Connectivity method for this forwarding rule (e.g. direct)
- `destinations` (String) Destination scope this forwarding rule applies to
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable a basic forwarding rule
- `name` (String) Basic forwarding rule name as an alphanumeric string [ 0-9a-zA-Z._ -]
- `user_locations` (String) User location scope this rule applies to



<a id="nestedatt--data--type--pac_file"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.pac_file`

Read-Only:

- `block_rule` (Attributes) Basic block rule configuration for PAC file and GlobalProtect proxy profiles (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule))
- `forwarding_rules` (Attributes List) List of PAC file-based forwarding rules (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--forwarding_rules))
- `pac_upload` (Boolean) User upload PAC file for PAC file based forwarding configuration

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.pac_file.block_rule`

Read-Only:

- `allow_tcp` (Attributes) TCP traffic allowlist configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule--allow_tcp))
- `allow_udp` (Attributes) UDP traffic allowlist configuration with location and destination support (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule--allow_udp))
- `enable` (Boolean) Enable block rule

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule--allow_tcp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.pac_file.block_rule.allow_tcp`

Read-Only:

- `enable_locations` (Boolean) Enable locations for allow-tcp
- `locations` (List of String) List of user locations allowed for TCP traffic


<a id="nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--block_rule--allow_udp"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.pac_file.block_rule.allow_udp`

Read-Only:

- `destinations` (String) Destination addresses or networks allowed for UDP traffic
- `enable_destinations` (Boolean) Enable destinations for allow-udp
- `enable_locations` (Boolean) Enable locations for allow-udp
- `locations` (List of String) List of user locations allowed for UDP traffic



<a id="nestedatt--data--type--pac_file--forwarding_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.pac_file.forwarding_rules`

Read-Only:

- `connectivity` (String) Connectivity method for this forwarding rule (e.g. direct)
- `destinations` (String) Destination scope this forwarding rule applies to
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable a basic forwarding rule
- `name` (String) Basic forwarding rule name as an alphanumeric string [ 0-9a-zA-Z._ -]
- `user_locations` (String) User location scope this rule applies to



<a id="nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.ztna_agent`

Read-Only:

- `block_rule` (Attributes) ZTNA block rule configuration (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent--block_rule))
- `forwarding_rules` (Attributes List) List of ZTNA agent-based forwarding rules (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent--forwarding_rules))
- `pac_upload` (Boolean) User uploaded PAC file for a ZTNA agent-based forwarding configuration

<a id="nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent--block_rule"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.ztna_agent.block_rule`

Read-Only:

- `allow_icmp_for_troubleshooting` (Boolean) Allow ICMP for troubleshooting
- `block_all_other_unmatched_outbound_connections` (Boolean) Block all other unmatched outbound connections
- `block_inbound_access_when_connected_to_tunnel` (Boolean) Block inbound access when connected to tunnel
- `block_non_tcp_non_udp_traffic_when_connected_to_tunnel` (Boolean) Block Non-TCP Non UDP based traffic when connected to tunnel
- `block_outbound_lan_access_when_connected_to_tunnel` (Boolean) Block outbound LAN access when connected to tunnel
- `enforcer_fqdn_dns_resolution_via_dns_servers` (Boolean) Enforce FQDN DNS resolution via tunnel DNS servers
- `resolve_all_fqdns_using_dns_servers_assigned_by_the_tunnel` (Boolean) Resolve All FQDNs using DNS servers assigned by the tunnel (Windows Only)


<a id="nestedatt--data--type--ztna_agent--forwarding_rules"></a>
### Nested Schema for `data.type.ztna_agent.forwarding_rules`

Read-Only:

- `connectivity` (String) Connectivity method for this ZTNA forwarding rule (e.g. direct)
- `destinations` (String) Destination scope this ZTNA forwarding rule applies to
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable a forwarding rule ztna
- `name` (String) Forwarding rule ZTNA name as an alphanumeric string [ 0-9a-zA-Z._ -]
- `source_applications` (String) Source applications this ZTNA rule applies to
- `traffic_type` (String) Type of traffic this ZTNA rule applies to (dns, network, or both)
- `user_locations` (String) User location scope this ZTNA rule applies to
